Trailer Visibility & Management

"Every Trailer, Every Day" — Building the foundation before we harvest the results
February 2026
A3-TR-2025-001
Kickoff: September 2025
Fred Staugh
Christina Wood
Kristi Noland
Carter Stocking
09/26/2025
12/31/2026
(A) Background
We manage roughly ~3,650 trailers across OTR, Dedicated, and Local operations, yet we lack reliable visibility into their status. Basic questions—location, load status, and reefer condition—often cannot be answered with confidence. This fragmentation leads to stale trailers, incorrect pickups, cargo claims, and dispatch teams operating without accurate data.
(B) Current State
📍 Foundational: “Where Is the Trailer?”
• GPS & AS400 data do not match
• Trailers not reporting / missing data
• Inconsistent beaming practices
• Overlapping or duplicate geofences
• Lost trailer process undocumented
• Telematics underutilized
🔄 Operational: “Trailer Status & Process Flow”
• Status fields used inconsistently (A/D/L/F)
• No standard process for drop vs. live load
• Trailer moves without dispatch
• Empty/arrival calls not entered timely
• Dual entry into multiple fields / systems
📊 Utilization: “Right Trailer, Right Place”
• Trailer-to-load imbalance by market
• Limited reporting on trailer pool efficiency
• Underuse of Manhattan planning tool
• Manual load planning & repositioning

(C) Problem Statement
We cannot consistently determine the true location, load status, or reefer condition of our trailers, creating operational fragmentation that results in stale trailers, incorrect pickups, cargo claims, and dispatch teams working without reliable data. Without a trustworthy, unified data foundation, downstream process improvements cannot be built.
(D) Root Cause
📍 Foundational
“Every Trailer, Every Day”
• Lack of system integration
• Manual updates & poor process discipline
• Multiple status fields & unclear logic
• Weak visibility & reporting framework
• Workflow design flaws in AS400
🔄 Operational Discipline
“Correct Trailer, Correct Status”
• Dispatch discipline gaps
• Poor training & unclear accountability
• Overreliance on tribal knowledge
📊 Utilization & Optimization
“Right Trailer, Right Place”
• Data quality not reliable enough for planning
• Lack of integration between Manhattan & AS400
• Resistance to using available tools
Core Finding: These systems were built independently over time. No one ever connected trailer location, status, and dispatch into a single, trustworthy data layer.
(E) Goal — Phase I
📡 Trailer Visibility
Improve trailer visibility from 64% (1,371 trailers with issues) to 100% daily visibility for every trailer, every day.
🔍 Lost Trailer Reduction
Reduce the number of lost trailers by 50%. Reduce lost trailers from 114 to 57 by July 31, 2026.
🚪 Door Sensor Installations
Expand door‑sensor coverage to improve visibility and security. Increase door‑sensor installations from 528 to 3,000 by July 31, 2026.
📉 Stale Trailer Reduction
Lower the percentage of stale trailers across the network. Reduce stale trailer percentage from 32.5% to 20% by December 31, 2026.
(F) Potential Countermeasures
We identified dozens of trailer-related opportunities. Rather than chase them all at once, we built a deliberate, phased strategy. You have to prepare the soil before you can harvest.
PHASE 1 Trailer Visibility: “Every Trailer, Every Day”
Objective: Establish accurate, real‑time visibility for all trailers.
Deliverables: Establish baselines for trailer management that don’t exist today, align GPS and AS400 records, resolve non‑reporting units, clean up geofences, recover lost trailers, redesign trailer reporting, enhance reefer status visibility, integrate rental trailers.
PHASE 2 Trailer Availability: “Correct Trailer, Correct Status”
Objective: Ensure trailer status accuracy and availability.
Deliverables: Implement trailer pool management, standardize status definitions, automate status triggers, establish weekly stale‑status targets, reactivate and improve auto‑dispatch.
PHASE 3 Utilization & Optimization: “Right Place, Right Time”
Objective: Optimize asset utilization once visibility and status accuracy are stabilized.
Deliverables: Balance trailer‑to‑load ratios, reactivate and improve auto‑dispatch, reduce empty miles, improve dwell reporting and exception visualization, redesign T‑call process.
PHASE 4 Dispatch Discipline & Process Adherence
Objective: Create consistent behaviors that sustain data accuracy and workflow integrity.
Deliverables: Train and hold dispatch accountable to standard processes, reinforce auto‑beaming and status accuracy checks, define “standard work” for trailer assignment and movement, close feedback loops between trailer management and dispatch.

(H) Validate Results & After Action Review
📡 Trailer Visibility
Visibility has improved from 64% to 94% daily trailer visibility.
🔍 Lost Trailer Reduction
Currently 47 trailers presumed lost; 8 recovered and 4 newly identified during the review.
🚪 Door Sensor Installations
As of 2/24/2025, door sensors have been installed on 2,675 trailers.
